svg.title: One architectural misuse, two symptoms, one fix
svg.desc: Two panels showing the same session information organized differently. Left: context window overloaded as memory system — rot attacks the middle of a bloated context, dementia at the /compact and /clear boundary. Right: context window small and clean as a workspace, with long-term memory externalized to a luminous belief-graph orb. Same information, different architecture. The left produces rot and dementia; the right makes both impossible.
svg.text: One architectural misuse · two symptoms · one fix
same session, same information — different place to keep it
everything crammed inside — nothing survives past /compact

g.text: tool output · file reads · bash dump · search results
fills 90%+ of context · mostly noise · can't be evicted selectively
every tool call eats tokens forever
user: what was our decision on retry logic?
asst: I think we were going to bump the timeout...

svg.text: structure dies at the boundary — nothing persists
small dense workspace · long-term state lives outside

g.text: user: what was our decision on retry?
asst: retry jitter — we rejected timeout bump

svg.text: context stays small · attention stays reliable
both symptoms active · both from the same cause
same information · different architecture · both impossible
github.com/immartian/bellamem · an applied instance of Recursive Emergence
